key_insights: Package-level documentation with comprehensive factory usage examples
Centralized imports from core.py module for easy access
Factory naming convention documentation (base and variant patterns)
Common usage patterns documented with code examples
Well-structured docstring explaining factory patterns and pytest-django integration
patterns: Grouping of factories by model type in imports
Documentation of factory methods (create, build, batch operations)

key_insights: Comprehensive factory-boy implementations for 9 core models
Multiple factory variants for each model (e.g., AdminUserFactory, StaffUserFactory, InvitedUserFactory for User)
Extensive use of LazyAttribute and LazyFunction for dynamic value generation
SubFactory patterns for creating related objects automatically
Custom _create method overrides for complex model creation logic
Post-generation hooks for password field in UserFactory
Proper Django model factory setup with skip_postgeneration_save
UUID-based unique value generation for preventing collisions
Faker integration for realistic test data
Plan assignment handling in OrganizationFactory and OrganizationWithPlanFactory
patterns: DjangoModelFactory base class for all factories
Meta class with model string reference and skip_postgeneration_save setting
SubFactory for automatic related object creation
Inheritance for factory variants with common setup
Post-generation for password setting with default value
Comprehensive docstrings with examples for each factory

patterns_discovered.description: Base factories extended by specialized variants (e.g., UserFactory → AdminUserFactory, StaffUserFactory, InvitedUserFactory)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Code reuse and consistent factory creation patterns across variants

patterns_discovered.description: Using uuid.uuid4().hex[:8] in LazyAttribute for unique, collision-free test data
patterns_discovered.benefit: Prevents test data conflicts and ensures uniqueness across test runs
patterns_discovered.frequency: Used extensively for name, slug, email fields across all factories

patterns_discovered.description: Automatic creation of related objects using factory.SubFactory
patterns_discovered.benefit: Eliminates boilerplate, ensures valid related objects exist when needed

patterns_discovered.description: Using @factory.post_generation decorator for password field setup
patterns_discovered.benefit: Allows flexible password setting with sensible defaults while maintaining security practices

patterns_discovered.description: Overriding _create method to handle plan assignment logic outside standard ORM
patterns_discovered.benefit: Handles complex creation logic not directly supported by model fields

patterns_discovered.description: Creating factories for different object states (ExpiredSessionFactory, RevokedSessionFactory)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Simplifies test setup for edge cases and different object states

patterns_discovered.description: Centralized documentation in __init__.py with usage examples and conventions
patterns_discovered.benefit: Clear API surface and guidance for test developers on factory usage

gotchas_discovered.description: While uuid4().hex[:8] is used for uniqueness, collision risk exists but is negligible for test purposes
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Using full UUID would eliminate risk, but hex[:8] is practical tradeoff for readability

gotchas_discovered.description: OrganizationFactory uses custom _create to handle plan assignment due to model constraints
gotchas_discovered.detail: Plan must be handled separately; OrganizationWithPlanFactory creates FREE plan via get_or_create
gotchas_discovered.impact: Requires understanding of plan relationship to properly use OrganizationWithPlanFactory

gotchas_discovered.description: Using skip_postgeneration_save=True means post_generation hooks must manually save if needed
gotchas_discovered.impact: Password hook only saves if create=True, may need careful handling in build scenarios

gotchas_discovered.description: PermissionFactory uses factory.Iterator for resource and action, which cycles through values
gotchas_discovered.limitation: May create duplicate permission combinations if not careful with batch creation
gotchas_discovered.workaround: Override in tests when unique permissions are needed

gotchas_discovered.description: Using LazyFunction for dict() vs LazyAttribute returning None requires understanding subtle differences
gotchas_discovered.location: settings = factory.LazyFunction(dict) vs @factory.lazy_attribute def logo(self): return None
gotchas_discovered.impact: Incorrect usage could lead to mutable default arguments or shared state

gotchas_discovered.description: UserFactory has organization=None by default, but many operations may require an organization
gotchas_discovered.impact: Tests must explicitly provide organization when needed
gotchas_discovered.recommendation: Document requirement clearly or consider SubFactory default

approach_outcome.description: Successfully created comprehensive test factories for core models using factory-boy
key_achievements: Implemented 9 primary factories (Organization, User, Branch, Role, Permission, Session, UserRole, RolePermission, AuditLog)
Created 17 factory variants for different object states and roles
Established consistent patterns using DjangoModelFactory base class
Integrated Faker library for realistic test data generation
Created custom logic for plan assignment and password handling
Provided comprehensive documentation with usage examples
approach_outcome.code_organization: Well-structured with clear separation of concerns
approach_outcome.documentation_level: Excellent - extensive docstrings and usage examples

recommendations.recommendation: Consider creating a base factory class to DRY up common patterns like UUID generation
recommendations.rationale: Would reduce boilerplate in _create methods and LazyAttribute definitions

recommendations.recommendation: Add factory tests to validate generated data meets model constraints
recommendations.rationale: Ensures factories always produce valid test data and catch model changes early

recommendations.recommendation: Create a TESTING.md guide documenting factory usage patterns specific to this project
recommendations.rationale: Would help new developers understand the factory ecosystem and best practices

recommendations.recommendation: Consider creating fixture factories for common test scenarios (e.g., complete user with roles and permissions)
recommendations.rationale: Would reduce boilerplate in test setup for common use cases

recommendations.recommendation: Document the decision to use skip_postgeneration_save=True and manual save() calls
recommendations.rationale: Ensures consistent understanding of save behavior across team

recommendations.recommendation: Add error handling in OrganizationWithPlanFactory for Plan model creation failures
recommendations.rationale: Current implementation assumes Plan model exists and get_or_create succeeds

recommendations.recommendation: Monitor factory creation performance in large batch operations (e.g., create_batch(1000))
recommendations.rationale: UUID generation and Faker could become bottleneck in heavy testing scenarios

recommendations.recommendation: Standardize use of LazyAttribute vs LazyFunction - document when to use each
recommendations.rationale: Current mixing of patterns could confuse developers about best usage
